pi_items.source: Web search confirmed .flipchart is the standard ActivInspire format extension (Promethean official format). Task description specifies this filename. Setup scripts create /home/ga/Documents/Flipcharts directory for storing these files.

setup_activinspire.sh:31 creates mkdir -p /home/ga/Documents/Flipcharts and sets DefaultPath=/home/ga/Documents/Flipcharts in config (line 48). This is the standard ActivInspire flipchart storage location. Both setup_task.sh and export_result.sh reference this exact path.

Task description explicitly specifies 3 pages: Page 1 (Title), Page 2 (Flat Surface/Object at Rest), Page 3 (Incline/Object on Ramp). Verifier.py checks pc >= 3 (line 110). Export script counts pages from flipchart XML structure.

Task description, Page 1 title: 'Free Body Diagrams'. Verified physics concept from Physics LibreTexts, Wikipedia, Khan Academy - Free Body Diagram is standard physics terminology. Export script checks for 'Free Body' text in XML (line 69).

Task description, Page 1 subtitle: 'Newton's Laws of Motion'. Newton's Laws are the foundational physics concept for Free Body Diagrams (verified from multiple physics education sources). Export script checks for 'Newton' text (line 70).
Task description, Page 2 title: 'Object at Rest'. This represents equilibrium state (Fnet=0) as verified in physics documentation. Export script checks for 'Rest' (line 71).

Task description specifies arrow labeled 'Fg' (Gravity). Web search (Physics LibreTexts, Khan Academy, Albert.io) confirms Fg is standard notation for gravitational force/weight in physics. Page 2 and Page 3 both require this label. Export script checks for 'Fg' (line 74).

Task description specifies arrow labeled 'Fn' (Normal Force). Web search (Pearson, Physics LibreTexts) confirms Fn is standard notation for normal force (perpendicular contact force). Page 2 and Page 3 require this. Verifier checks this critically (line 128). Export script checks for 'Fn' (line 75).
Task description specifies text 'Fnet = 0' on Page 2. Web search confirms Fnet is standard notation for net force in physics. Export script checks for 'Fnet' (line 77).
Task description, Page 3 title: 'Object on Ramp'. Physics education sources (A+ Physics, Khan Academy) use 'ramp' for inclined plane terminology. Export script checks for 'Ramp' (line 72).

Task description specifies arrow labeled 'Ff' (Friction) on Page 3. Web search (Physics LibreTexts, Pearson, Khan Academy) confirms Ff is standard notation for friction force. Friction appears on inclined plane diagrams pointing along the surface. Export script checks for 'Ff' (line 76).

Task description, Page 3: 'Draw a large triangle (ramp)'. Export script checks for Triangle shape in XML (line 81 checks for type="Triangle" or AsTriangle or <Triangle). Verifier.py checks for this (line 133).

Task description, Page 2 and 3: 'Draw a rectangle (box)'. Export script checks for Rectangle shape in XML (line 86 checks for type="Rectangle" or AsRectangle or <Rectangle). Verifier.py checks for this (line 139).

pi_items.metadata_value: Fg pointing down, Fn pointing up, Fnet=0
pi_items.source: Web search on Free Body Diagrams (Physics LibreTexts, University Physics Volume 1) confirms that for an object at rest on a surface: gravity pulls straight down (Fg), normal force perpendicular to surface pointing up (Fn), and net force equals zero (Fnet=0). This is Newton's First Law (equilibrium).

pi_items.metadata_value: Fg straight down, Fn perpendicular to ramp, Ff up the ramp
pi_items.source: Web search on inclined planes (Khan Academy, Pearson, A+ Physics, Texas Gateway) confirms: gravity acts vertically downward regardless of ramp angle (Fg), normal force is perpendicular to the ramp surface (Fn), and friction acts along the surface opposing motion (Ff pointing up the ramp). This requires proper coordinate system understanding for inclined planes.

Web search confirms ActivInspire is real interactive whiteboard software by Promethean. Setup scripts reference /usr/bin/activinspire and /usr/local/bin/activsoftware/Inspire. Software is designed for educators to create interactive lesson presentations.

pi_items.metadata_value: Flipchart files are ZIP archives containing XML
pi_items.verified_value: Flipchart files are ZIP archives with XML content
pi_items.source: Task utilities (task_utils.sh:51-52) check file type for 'zip' or 'archive'. Export script (export_result.sh:64) uses 'unzip' to extract content. Verifier documentation states flipchart files contain XML data. Web search on FLIPCHART format confirms these are compressed archive files.

pi_items.metadata_value: Rectangle must be rotated to sit flush against ramp
pi_items.verified_value: Rotation is required for correct physics representation
pi_items.source: Task description explicitly states: 'ROTATE the rectangle so it sits flush against the ramp.' This is correct physics - the box must be aligned with the ramp surface. Export script checks for rotation via angle attributes (line 94). Verifier uses VLM to visually verify 'box_is_rotated_to_match_ramp' (line 151).

privileged_info_summary: This is a physics lesson creation task using ActivInspire (real Promethean software). The task requires creating a 3-page flipchart about Free Body Diagrams and Newton's Laws. All required text elements (Free Body, Newton, Rest, Fg, Fn, Fnet, Ramp, Ff) and shapes (Triangle for ramp, Rectangle for object) are specified and consistent. The physics concepts are accurate: Fg is gravitational force (always pointing down), Fn is normal force (perpendicular to surface), and Ff is friction (parallel to surface, opposing motion). Page 2 shows equilibrium (Fnet=0), and Page 3 requires the object to be rotated to align with the ramp surface. The file format (.flipchart) is the standard ActivInspire format, which is a ZIP archive containing XML. All specifications are internally consistent across task description, verification logic, and setup scripts.
